description: Establish the visual foundation and structural shell for Stackwise. This includes updating the CSS design system with a teal/amber color palette using oklch, importing Geist Sans and Geist Mono fonts, creating the authenticated app layout with a fixed 260px sidebar (grouped navigation sections for Operations, Procurement, Requests, Intelligence, Admin), a persistent top header bar with CMD+K search trigger and quick-action button, and defining all route stubs. The shell must be responsive — sidebar collapses on mobile. All subsequent features build inside this shell.

stories.title: Update CSS design tokens with teal/amber palette
stories.description: Replace the default shadcn color values in src/styles.css :root and .dark blocks with the Stackwise teal/amber palette from design-guidelines.md. Add custom tokens for --stock-healthy, --stock-low, --stock-out, --amber-accent. Update the @theme inline block to register any new tokens. All values must use oklch format.
acceptanceCriteria: All :root color variables use oklch values matching design-guidelines.md
Dark mode (.dark) has appropriately inverted/adjusted values
--stock-healthy (green), --stock-low (amber), --stock-out (red) tokens exist
--amber-accent token registered for CTA/warning use
Border radius set to 6px (--radius: 0.375rem)
No hardcoded colors in the file — all semantic tokens

verification.test: Open the app at /. Inspect :root CSS variables in devtools. Confirm --primary uses oklch(0.55 0.17 162), --background uses oklch(0.985 0.005 80). Toggle .dark on html element and confirm dark mode tokens apply. Zero console errors.

stories.notes: Verified: all oklch tokens applied, --primary=oklch(0.55 0.17 162), --background=oklch(0.985 0.005 80), stock/amber tokens present, radius=0.375rem.

stories.title: Add Geist Sans and Geist Mono fonts
stories.description: Import Geist Sans and Geist Mono web fonts. Configure them in the CSS so Geist Sans is the default body/UI font and Geist Mono is available via a utility class (font-mono). Update the html element in __root.tsx to apply the base font family.
acceptanceCriteria: Geist Sans loads and renders as the default body font
Geist Mono is available via the font-mono Tailwind class
Fonts load without FOUT (font-display: swap or preload)
Both fonts render correctly on the placeholder page at /
No console errors related to font loading

verification.test: Open /. Inspect the body element — computed font-family should show Geist Sans. Add a temporary element with class font-mono — it should render in Geist Mono. No 404s in network tab for font files. Zero console errors.

stories.notes: Variable woff2 fonts in public/fonts/. Body renders Geist Sans, font-mono renders Geist Mono.

stories.title: Create TypeScript types for inventory domain
stories.description: Create src/types/inventory.ts with all core TypeScript interfaces: Item, Category, Supplier, Location, StockMovement, PurchaseOrder, PurchaseOrderItem, InventoryRequest, RequestItem, CustomFieldDefinition, Notification, UserRole. Include enums for MovementType, OrderStatus, RequestStatus, ItemStatus, UserRoleType. These types will be used across all features.
acceptanceCriteria: All interfaces defined with proper field types
Enums for MovementType (received, shipped, adjusted, transferred)
Enums for OrderStatus (draft, submitted, partial, received, cancelled)
Enums for RequestStatus (pending, approved, fulfilled, declined)
Enums for ItemStatus (active, discontinued, archived)
Enums for UserRoleType (admin, manager, requestor)
File exports all types and enums
File is under 250 lines

verification.test: Import any type from src/types/inventory.ts in a temporary file. TypeScript should compile without errors. No circular dependencies. Zero console errors on page load.

stories.notes: 174 lines. All interfaces and enums exported. Zero console errors.

stories.title: Create app layout route with sidebar and header
stories.description: Create src/routes/app.tsx as a layout route with <Outlet />. It renders a fixed 260px Sidebar on the left and a Header bar at top of the content area. The main content area fills remaining width with 32px padding on desktop, 16px on mobile. Create src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx and src/components/layout/Header.tsx as separate files. Sidebar has the Stackwise logo/wordmark at top, then grouped nav sections: Operations (Dashboard, Catalog, Movements), Procurement (Suppliers, Purchase Orders), Requests, Intelligence (Analytics, AI Insights), Admin (Settings). Each nav item uses a Lucide icon + label. Active route is highlighted with teal background. Header has a search trigger button (displays 'Search... ⌘K'), a quick-action '+' button, and a user avatar placeholder on the right.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout route at /app renders sidebar + header + outlet
Sidebar is exactly 260px wide on desktop, fixed position
Sidebar has Stackwise wordmark at top
Nav items grouped under section labels with Lucide icons
Active nav item has teal highlight background
Header spans the content area with search trigger and action button
Main content has 32px padding desktop, 16px mobile
On mobile (< 768px), sidebar is hidden by default
All colors use semantic tokens, no hardcoded values
Both Sidebar and Header files are under 250 lines each

verification.test: Navigate to /app. Sidebar should be visible on left at 260px wide with all navigation groups. Header bar at top with search and action buttons. Resize to 390px — sidebar should be hidden. Zero console errors.

stories.notes: Sidebar 260px, all nav groups, Stackwise wordmark, header with search+action+avatar. Mobile: sidebar hidden, hamburger visible. Zero console errors.

stories.title: Create all app route stubs
stories.description: Create route files for every app page as stubs (page title + 'Coming soon' placeholder). Files: src/routes/app.dashboard.tsx (/app/dashboard), src/routes/app.catalog.tsx (/app/catalog), src/routes/app.movements.tsx (/app/movements), src/routes/app.suppliers.tsx (/app/suppliers), src/routes/app.purchase-orders.tsx (/app/purchase-orders), src/routes/app.requests.tsx (/app/requests), src/routes/app.analytics.tsx (/app/analytics), src/routes/app.ai-insights.tsx (/app/ai-insights), src/routes/app.settings.tsx (/app/settings). Each stub renders a centered heading with the page name. The /app route should redirect to /app/dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 route files exist and are valid TanStack routes
Each renders a heading with the feature name
Navigating to /app redirects to /app/dashboard
Each route is accessible via sidebar navigation links
No TypeScript errors in any route file
Route types auto-generate correctly in routeTree.gen.ts

verification.test: Navigate to /app — should redirect to /app/dashboard. Click each sidebar nav item in sequence: Catalog, Movements, Suppliers, Purchase Orders, Requests, Analytics, AI Insights, Settings. Each should load its stub page with the correct heading. Active nav item should highlight. No console errors on any page.

stories.notes: All 9 routes created. /app redirects to /app/dashboard via Navigate component. All sidebar links work. Zero console errors.

stories.title: Sidebar mobile toggle with hamburger menu
stories.description: On screens below 768px, the sidebar should be hidden by default and accessible via a hamburger menu button in the Header. Clicking the hamburger opens the sidebar as an overlay (Sheet component sliding from left). Clicking a nav item closes the sidebar. Clicking outside closes it. Add the hamburger button to Header.tsx and wire it with state. Use the Sheet component from shadcn.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar hidden on screens < 768px
Hamburger icon visible in header on mobile
Clicking hamburger opens sidebar as left-sliding sheet overlay
Clicking a nav item closes the mobile sidebar
Clicking overlay backdrop closes the sidebar
Desktop sidebar remains fixed and always visible
Smooth 300ms slide animation on open/close

verification.test: Resize viewport to 390px width. Confirm sidebar is hidden. Hamburger icon should be visible in header. Click hamburger — sidebar slides in from left as overlay. Click 'Catalog' nav item — sidebar closes and page changes to catalog. Reopen sidebar, click outside — sidebar closes. Resize to 1200px — sidebar is always visible, no hamburger icon.

stories.notes: Already implemented in US-01-004. Hamburger opens Sheet from left, nav click closes it, click-outside closes it. Desktop sidebar always visible. Zero errors.

stories.title: Sidebar nav section collapse/expand
stories.description: Each nav group in the sidebar (Operations, Procurement, Intelligence, Admin) should be collapsible. Clicking the section header toggles its children visible/hidden with a smooth 150ms height animation. A small chevron icon rotates to indicate state. All sections default to expanded. Collapse state persists only during the session (no localStorage needed).
acceptanceCriteria: Each nav group header is clickable to collapse/expand
Chevron icon rotates 90° on collapse
Smooth 150ms height transition animation
All sections start expanded by default
Collapsed sections hide their child nav items
Requests link (ungrouped) is always visible
Collapse state resets on page refresh (session only)

verification.test: Navigate to /app/dashboard. Click 'Operations' section header — Dashboard, Catalog, Movements items should animate closed. Chevron should rotate. Click again — items expand back. Repeat for Procurement, Intelligence, Admin sections. Requests link should always be visible (not in a collapsible group).

stories.notes: Collapse/expand works for all 4 groups. Chevron rotates. Requests always visible. Conditional render instead of max-h trick for proper visibility. Zero errors.

stories.title: StatusBadge shared component
stories.description: Create src/components/StatusBadge.tsx — a reusable badge component for stock status display. Accepts a status prop: 'in-stock' | 'low-stock' | 'out-of-stock'. Renders a small colored dot + label text. Uses the --stock-healthy, --stock-low, --stock-out tokens. 'low-stock' dot should have a subtle CSS pulse animation. Component also supports item statuses: 'active', 'discontinued', 'archived' with appropriate muted styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Component renders colored dot + text label
in-stock: green dot, 'In Stock' label
low-stock: amber dot with CSS pulse, 'Low Stock' label
out-of-stock: solid red dot, 'Out of Stock' label
active/discontinued/archived variants with muted colors
Uses semantic CSS tokens, not hardcoded colors
Component is under 80 lines
Exported and importable from src/components/StatusBadge

verification.test: Temporarily render all StatusBadge variants on the dashboard stub page. Confirm: green dot for in-stock, pulsing amber for low-stock, red for out-of-stock. Active/discontinued/archived render with muted styling. No console errors.

stories.notes: 55 lines. All 6 variants render correctly. Pulse animation on low-stock. Semantic tokens only. Zero console errors.
